title: triticum aestivum subsp. spelta
intro: Triticum aestivum subsp. spelta, also known as spelt, is a subspecies of wheat that has been cultivated for thousands of years for its nutritious and medicinal properties. It is rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals, particularly iron, selenium, and manganese, which contribute to its health benefits and medicinal properties. Spelt is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, flour, or supplement, and is often used in traditional medicine and as a dietary addition to promote overall well-being.
